taxonomy: Euosma nuda is classified within the family Chrysididae, commonly known as the jewel wasps, which are a group of small wasps. The Chrysididae family is part of the order Hymenoptera, which also includes ants, bees, and sawflies. The order Hymenoptera is further divided into several suborders, with Chrysididae belonging to the Aculeata suborder. Euosma nuda specifically belongs to the genus Euosma, which is a group of wasps found in various parts of the world. The classification of Euosma nuda is an example of how species are grouped and named based on their characteristics and evolutionary relationships.
